flashcards.q: Why are transport and communication called 'lifelines' of an economy?
flashcards.a: Because the pace of development depends on the production of goods/services and their efficient movement over space.

flashcards.q: What is the total length of India's road network (2020–21)?
Which organisation implements the Golden Quadrilateral Super Highways?
Which two extreme locations are connected by the North-South corridor?
Which cities are connected by the East-West corridor?
Who maintains the National Highways in India?
What is the historical name of the road between Delhi and Amritsar?
Under which scheme did rural roads receive a special impetus?
When was the Border Roads Organisation (BRO) established?
What is the length of the Atal Tunnel?
The Atal Tunnel connects Manali to which valley?
Which range of the Himalayas is the Atal Tunnel built in?
What is the difference between metalled and unmetalled roads?
flashcards.a: Metalled roads are all-weather (cement/bitumen); unmetalled roads go out of use in rainy seasons.

flashcards.q: In which year did the first train run in India?
The Indian Railway is currently reorganized into how many zones?
What are the three types of railway gauges in India?
flashcards.a: Broad Gauge, Metre Gauge, and Narrow Gauge.

flashcards.q: Which region of India is most favourable for the railway network?
flashcards.a: The Northern Plains (due to level land and high population density).

flashcards.q: Which railway development has facilitated movement along India's west coast?
State one major advantage of pipeline transport.
flashcards.a: It rules out trans-shipment losses or delays.

flashcards.q: Name the first cross-country gas pipeline in India.
What is the total length of India's inland navigation waterways?
Which river stretch is known as National Waterway No. 1?
flashcards.a: Ganga river between Prayagraj and Haldia.

flashcards.q: Where is National Waterway No. 2 located?
What is the length of India's coastline?
Which tidal port was developed soon after Independence to handle trade for North-west India?
Which is the biggest port in India with a spacious natural harbour?
Which port specializes in the export of iron ore from Kudremukh mines?
Which is the oldest artificial port of India?
Which port is known as the deepest landlocked and well-protected port?
Which port is an inland riverine port serving the Ganga-Brahmaputra basin?
Which organisation provides helicopter services for off-shore oil operations?
What is the primary goal of the UDAN scheme?
flashcards.a: To promote regional connectivity by making flying affordable.

flashcards.q: Which mail category is airlifted between stations in India?
How many mail channels have been introduced recently in large cities?
What does STD stand for in telecommunications?
What is the 'economic barometer' of a country?
Define 'Favourable Balance of Trade'.
flashcards.a: When the value of exports exceeds the value of imports.

flashcards.q: Name three categories of tourism in India.
flashcards.a: Heritage, Eco, Adventure, Medical, and business tourism.

flashcards.q: Name the authority that certifies both Indian and foreign films.
Which language has the largest number of newspapers published in India?
What is the focus of the Digital India programme?
flashcards.a: Transforming India into a knowledge-based economy through technology.

flashcards.q: What is 'hinterland'?
flashcards.a: The remote or less developed parts of a country behind a coast or river bank.

flashcards.q: Which port was developed as a subsidiary to relieve pressure on Kolkata port?
True or False: Roadways are more expensive to build than railways in hilly terrain.
flashcards.a: False. Roadways are much cheaper and can traverse steeper slopes.
